items.title: Building GenAI Platform at DoorDash
items.speaker: Siddharth Kodwani / Sudeep Das (DoorDash)
items.description: How DoorDash built internal GenAI infrastructure to support AI capabilities across engineering teams at scale — retries, fallbacks, prompt versioning, cost tracking, and trust/safety checks in a real-time 3-sided marketplace. Engineering deep-dive on the internal developer platform that makes AI features reliable, observable, and cost-controlled in production

items.title: Beyond Prompting: Context Engineering for Production-Grade AI
items.speaker: QCon AI Boston 2026 Speaker
items.description: Context engineering as an architectural discipline — treating the LLM context window as a first-class resource to design, optimize, and manage. Covers KV cache prefix reuse, memory hierarchies for long multi-turn sessions, tool result compression, retrieval-augmented context injection, and why disciplined context management delivers more consistent task success than prompt engineering alone

items.title: Capacity Efficiency at Meta: How Unified AI Agents Optimize Performance at Hyperscale
items.speaker: Meta Engineering
items.description: Meta's AI agent platform for autonomous infrastructure capacity optimization — how MCP Tools and encoded engineer Skills power offense (proactive savings discovery) and defense (AI Regression Solver auto-detects regressions and generates PRs), recovering hundreds of megawatts of compute. Covers the architecture, safety boundaries, and how the same foundation scaled from one efficiency application to an entire agentic program. Published April 28, 2026.

items.title: Serving LLMs at Scale: the Hidden KV Cache Advantage
items.speaker: Khawaja Shams (Co-Founder & CEO, Momento)
items.description: KV cache as the hidden lever behind inference cost and latency: how GPU utilization, TTFB, and throughput are dominated by prefix-cache hit rates. Covers practical techniques for maximizing KV reuse in production LLM serving — shared prefix management, prefix-aware routing, and the economics of on-GPU vs CPU-offloaded KV storage.

items.title: Beyond the Prototype: Scaling Framework Agnostic AI Agent Infrastructure with Ray
items.speaker: Deepak Chandramouli & Bhumik Thakkar (Apple)
items.description: Moving from a local notebook to a production-grade Agent Engine at Apple scale. Demonstrates a unified deployment layer that is agnostic to agent framework (LangGraph, AutoGen, custom), using Ray Serve as the reference implementation to handle unpredictable execution times, long-running agentic loops, and framework lock-in. Architecture patterns for multi-agent orchestration serving billions of users.

items.title: Beyond vLLM: Distributed LLM Inferencing With llm-d on Kubernetes
items.speaker: Ravindra Patil (Red Hat)
items.description: Introduction to llm-d, the CNCF Sandbox project jointly donated by IBM Research, Red Hat, and Google Cloud (with NVIDIA, CoreWeave, AMD, Cisco as founding partners). Demonstrates Kubernetes-native disaggregated LLM serving beyond single-node vLLM: hierarchical KV cache offloading (VRAM → DRAM → NVMe → network), prefix-cache-aware routing using the same Branch-Sharded KV Indexer approach as NVIDIA Dynamo, and the llm-d CRD API for declarative inference deployments. Positions llm-d as a middleware layer in the Kubernetes AI Conformance Program's inference tier.

items.title: Your AI Agent Has No SLO — Defining Reliability for Autonomous Systems
items.speaker: Andrew Espira (Kustode), AI SRE Summit panel
items.description: Addresses the operational gap between traditional SRE (distributed tracing, SLIs, SLOs, error budgets on deterministic services) and AI agents that auto-remediate incidents. Explores how to define agent-observed SLIs (task completion rate, step efficiency, tool-call accuracy, human escalation rate), calibrate autonomy with safety gates and human-in-the-loop checkpoints, and set error budgets for non-deterministic agentic workflows. Features a panel including Charity Majors (Honeycomb), Stefana Muller (Salesforce VP Infrastructure), and Itiel Shwartz (Komodor CTO).

items.title: Team Topologies as the 'Infrastructure for Agency' with AI
items.speaker: Matthew Skelton (co-author, Team Topologies)
items.description: Argues that AI success depends on organisational maturity, not just model capability. Maps cognitive load directly to AI context windows: just as humans fail when mental capacity is exceeded, AI agents hallucinate or lose coherence when they operate outside defined boundaries. Introduces stewardship (vs. mere ownership) as the production operating model for AI systems — looking after agentic infrastructure for those who follow. JP Morgan Athena platform case study: reduced 60% of dependencies using opt-in adoption ('friendly FOMO') rather than top-down mandates — social dynamics outperformed enforcement. Innovation and Practices Enabling Teams as the mechanism for knowledge diffusion and measurable AI ROI. Core thesis: the primary obstacles to AI value are organisational, not technical — team structure is the hidden constraint.

items.title: Blurring the Lines: Engineering & Data Teams in the Age of AI
items.speaker: Lada Indra (Head of Data Platform, Pleo)
items.description: AI systems have erased the boundary between software engineering and data engineering: engineers now deploy models, handle streaming predictions, and manage real-time feature pipelines; data engineers now own production-grade systems with engineer-level SLAs and on-call rotation. Practical convergence strategies: (1) data contracts — treat data streams like APIs by defining ownership, schema, and SLAs in code enforced through CI/CD schema registries, preventing silent breaks from corrupting downstream ML features; (2) full-stack observability — extend monitoring beyond uptime/latency to include data health (semantic validity, freshness, distribution consistency); (3) joint incident management — analytics engineers and data scientists join architecture reviews and share on-call responsibilities. Conclusion: the AI era requires one system, one team, shared responsibility — dissolving silos is the only way to deliver AI features reliably at scale.

items.title: The Big Kubernetes Shift — Inside KubeCon 2026
items.speaker: Various
items.description: Comprehensive KubeCon EU 2026 recap covering the major themes: Kubernetes AI Conformance Program, CNCF Sandbox projects for inference (llm-d, KAI GPU scheduler), OpenTelemetry consolidation, and the shift toward AI-native platform engineering. Good 30-minute orientation for the 2026 cloud-native landscape.
